Plans: Will I have to file my own claims?
TRICARE Prime: Your provider will file claims for you (in most cases).
TRICARE Prime Remote: Your provider will file claims for you (in most cases).
TRICARE Select: Network providers will file claims for you. If you get care from a non-network provider, you may have to file your own claims.
TRICARE Prime Overseas: Your provider will file claims for you (in most cases).
TRICARE Prime Remote Overseas: Your providers will file claims for you (in most cases).
TRICARE Select Overseas: You may be required to pay for care up front and file claims with Wisconsin Physicians Service, the TRICARE Overseas claims processor.
TRICARE Reserve Select: If you receive care from a network provider, your provider will submit claims on your behalf. If you receive care from a non-network provider, you may be required to submit your own health care claims.
TRICARE Retired Reserve: Network providers will file claims for you. If you get care from a non-network provider, you may have to file your own claims.
TRICARE For Life: Not usually. In most cases, your provider will file the claims with Medicare.  Medicare will pay its portion and automatically forward the claim electronically to TRICARE for processing.
TRICARE Young Adult - Select: Network providers will file claims for you. If you get care from a non-network provider, you may have to file your own claims.
TRICARE Young Adult - Prime Option: Your provider will file claims for you (in most cases).
TRICARE Young Adult - Prime Option (Over: Your provider will file claims for you (in most cases).
TRICARE Young Adult - Prime Option (Prim: Your provider will file claims for you (in most cases).
TRICARE Young Adult - Prime Option (USFH: There are no claims to file when you see providers approved by the plan.
US Family Health Plan: There are no claims to file when you see providers approved by the plan.
TRICARE Prime Demo: There are no claims to file when you see providers approved by the plan.

TRICARE Prime: In the United States in Prime Service Areas.
TRICARE Prime Remote: In designated remote U.S. locations, usually more than 50 miles or one hour's drive time, from a military hospital or clinic.
TRICARE Prime Overseas: In overseas areas near military hospitals and clinics.
TRICARE Prime Remote Overseas: In designated remote overseas locations.      *Eurasia-Africa     *Latin America and Canada     *Pacific
TRICARE For Life: Worldwide. In areas where Medicare does not provide coverage, TRICARE is the primary payer. Select TRICARE For Life (while in an overseas area that doesn't have Medicare)" to compare."
TRICARE Young Adult - Prime Option: Prime Service Areas in the United States.
TRICARE Young Adult - Prime Option (Prim: Designated remote locations in the United States—usually 50 miles or an hour's drive time from a military hosptial or clinic.
TRICARE Young Adult - Prime Option (USFH: The US Family Health Plan is available through not-for-profit health care systems in designated Zip codes in six areas of the country:  *Brighton Marine Health Center: Serving Massachusetts (including Cape Cod), northern Connecticut, southern New Hampshire and Rhode Island  *CHRISTUS Health: Serving southeast Texas and southwest Louisiana  *Johns Hopkins Medicine: Serving central Maryland, Washington DC and parts of Pennsylvania, Virginia and West Virginia  *Martin's Point Health Care: Serving Maine, Vermont, New Hampshire and northeastern New York  *PacMed Clinics: Serving the Puget Sound area of Washington State  *Saint Vincent Catholic Medical Centers: Serving parts of New York, all of New Jersey, eastern Pennsylvania and southern Connecticut
US Family Health Plan: The US Family Health Plan is available through not-for-profit health care systems in designated Zip codes in six areas of the country:  *Brighton Marine Health Center: Serving Massachusetts (including Cape Cod), northern Connecticut, southern New Hampshire and Rhode Island  *CHRISTUS Health: Serving southeast Texas and southwest Louisiana  *Johns Hopkins Medicine: Serving central Maryland, Washington DC and parts of Pennsylvania, Virginia and West Virginia  *Martin's Point Health Care: Serving Maine, Vermont, New Hampshire and northeastern New York  *PacMed Clinics: Serving the Puget Sound area of Washington State  *Saint Vincent Catholic Medical Centers: Serving parts of New York, all of New Jersey, eastern Pennsylvania and southern Connecticut
TRICARE Prime Demo: Available in these areas through CareSource Military and Veterans: •	Atlanta, Georgia •	Tampa, Florida

Plans: Do I have to enroll? What is the annual fee?
TRICARE Prime: *Enrollment is required.     *No enrollment fee for active duty families.      *To see costs, check out TRICARE Costs.
TRICARE Prime Remote: *Enrollment is required.     *To see costs, check out TRICARE Costs.
TRICARE Select: Enrollment is required. To see costs, check out TRICARE Costs.
TRICARE Prime Overseas: *Enrollment is required.     *To see costs, check out TRICARE Costs.
TRICARE Prime Remote Overseas: *Enrollment is required.     *To see costs, check out TRICARE Costs.
TRICARE Select Overseas: Enrollment is required. To see costs, check out TRICARE Costs.
TRICARE Reserve Select: To see costs, check out TRICARE Costs.
TRICARE Retired Reserve: To see costs, check out TRICARE Costs.
TRICARE For Life: No, but you must have Medicare Part A & B to participate.
TRICARE Young Adult - Select: To see costs, check out TRICARE Costs.
TRICARE Young Adult - Prime Option: To see costs, check out TRICARE Costs.
TRICARE Young Adult - Prime Option (Over: To see costs, check out TRICARE Costs.
TRICARE Young Adult - Prime Option (Prim: To see costs, check out TRICARE Costs.
TRICARE Young Adult - Prime Option (USFH: To see costs, check out TRICARE Costs.
US Family Health Plan: *Enrollment is required and includes a one-year commitment to receive care from the plan, unless you move to an area where the plan is not offered.     *To see costs, check out TRICARE Costs.
TRICARE Prime Demo: •	Enrollment is required. (If you move to an area where the plan isn’t offered, you’ll be disenrolled from the plan.) •	No enrollment fee for active duty families and transitional survivors. •	For all others, no enrollment fee for the first 12 months of enrollment. After the first year, you’ll pay TRICARE Prime enrollment fees. •	To see costs, check out TRICARE Costs.

TRICARE Prime: Get most care from your assigned PCM.       *Military or network provider     *Refers you to specialists for care he or she can't provide
TRICARE Prime Remote: Get most care from your PCM; refers you to specialists for care he or she can't provide.      *Network provider, if available     *If not, any TRICARE-authorized provider can be your PCM
TRICARE Select: *Get care from any TRICARE-authorized provider, network or non-network.      *Referrals not required.     *Some services may require prior authorization.
TRICARE Prime Overseas: *Get most care from your PCM at a military hospital or clinic.      *Your PCM refers you to specialists for care he or she can't provide
TRICARE Prime Remote Overseas: The overseas contractor will coordinate all your care with approved overseas providers. All care is coordinated through the regional call centers.
TRICARE Select Overseas: *Get care from any qualified overseas provider.     *Referrals not required.     *Some services may require prior authorization.     *If you live near a military hospital or clinic, you must have a non-availability statement before receiving inpatient care at an overseas facility.
TRICARE Reserve Select: You may get care from any TRICARE-authorized provider, network or non-network, without a referral. Some services require pre-authorization.
TRICARE Retired Reserve: *Get care from any TRICARE-authorized provider, network or non-network.      *Referrals not required.     *Some services may require prior authorization.
TRICARE For Life: Make an appointment with any authorized provider.
TRICARE Young Adult - Select: *Get care from any TRICARE-authorized provider, network or non-network.      *Referrals not required.     *Some services may require prior authorization.
TRICARE Young Adult - Prime Option: Get most care from your assigned PCM.       *Military or network provider     *Refers you to specialists for care he or she can't provide
TRICARE Young Adult - Prime Option (Over: *Get most care from your PCM at a military hospital or clinic.      *Your PCM refers you to specialists for care he or she can't provide     *If you live in a remote location overseas, all care including routine care is coordinated through your regional call center
TRICARE Young Adult - Prime Option (Prim: Get most care from your PCM; refers you to specialists for care he or she can't provide.      *Network provider, if available     *If not, any TRICARE-authorized provider can be your PCM
TRICARE Young Adult - Prime Option (USFH: You get care all care from your designated US Family Health Plan provider. You will not be able to access care from Medicare providers, military hospitals and clinics, or TRICARE-authorized providers.
US Family Health Plan: You get care all care from your designated US Family Health Plan provider. You will not be able to access care from Medicare providers, military hospitals and clinics, or TRICARE-authorized providers.
TRICARE Prime Demo: •	Get care from your plan’s network of primary and specialty care providers. •	Referrals aren’t required. But some services may require pre-authorization. •	You won’t be able to use military hospitals or clinics, except in an emergency.

TRICARE Prime: *Enrollment required     *Enhanced vision coverage and preventive services     *Most care received from your primary care manager      *Time and distance access standards     *Fewer out-of-pocket costs     *No claims to file (in most cases)
TRICARE Prime Remote: *Enrollment required     *Enhanced vision coverage and preventive services     *May or may not have an assigned primary care manager     *Time and distance access standards     *Fewer out-of-pocket costs     *No claims to file (in most cases)
TRICARE Select: *Enrollment Required     *Get care from any TRICARE-authorized provider, network or non-network      *Referrals not required, but some care may require prior authorization      *You may have to pay for services up front and file your own claims for reimbursement
TRICARE Prime Overseas: *Enrollment required     *Enhanced vision coverage and preventive services     *Most care received from your primary care manager     *Time and distance access standards     *Fewer out-of-pocket costs     *No claims to file (in most cases)
TRICARE Prime Remote Overseas: *Enrollment required        *Enhanced vision coverage and preventive services      *All care coordinated through regional call centers     *Time and distance access standards       *Fewer out-of-pocket costs       *No claims to file (in most cases)
TRICARE Select Overseas: *Enrollment required     *Get care from any TRICARE-authorized provider, network or non-network      *Referrals not required, but some care may require prior authorization      *You may have to pay for services up front and file your own claims for reimbursement
TRICARE Reserve Select: *Must qualify for and purchase the plan to participate      *Receive care from any TRICARE-authorized provider, network or non-network      *No referrals required, but some care may require prior authorization      *Costs vary depending on type of provider seen; fewer out-of-pocket costs from TRICARE network providers      *May have to pay for services when they are received and submit health care claims for reimbursement
TRICARE Retired Reserve: *Must qualify for and purchase the plan to participate      *Get care from any TRICARE-authorized provider, network or non-network     *Referrals not required, but some care may require prior authorization     *You may have to pay for services up front and file your own claims for reimbursement
TRICARE For Life: *Medicare Part A & B required      *Get care from any authorized provider      *Minimal out-of-pocket costs (aside from Medicare Part B premium)      *No claims to file (in most cases)
TRICARE Young Adult - Select: *Must qualify for and purchase the plan to participate      *Get care from any TRICARE-authorized provider, network or non-network     *Referrals not required, but some care may require prior authorization     *You may have to pay for services up front and file your own claims for reimbursement
TRICARE Young Adult - Prime Option: *Must qualify for and purchase TRICARE Young Adult to participate     *Enhanced vision coverage and preventive services     *Most care received from your primary care manager     *Time and distance access standards     *Fewer out-of-pocket costs     *No claims to file (in most cases)
TRICARE Young Adult - Prime Option (Over: *Must qualify for and purchase TRICARE Young Adult to participate     *Enhanced vision coverage and preventive services     *Most care received from your primary care manager      *Time and distance access standards     *Fewer out-of-pocket costs     *No claims to file (in most cases)
TRICARE Young Adult - Prime Option (Prim: *Must qualify for and purchase TRICARE Young Adult to participate *Only available to children of active duty service members enrolled in TRICARE Prime Remote *Most care received from your primary care manager *No claims to file (in most cases)
TRICARE Young Adult - Prime Option (USFH: *Must qualify for and purchase TRICARE Young Adult to participate     *Enhanced vision coverage and preventive services     *Most care received from your primary care manager      *Fewer out-of-pocket costs     *No claims to file (in most cases)
US Family Health Plan: *Enrollment required     *Enhanced vision coverage and preventive services     *Most care received from your primary care manager      *Fewer out-of-pocket costs     *No claims to file (in most cases)
TRICARE Prime Demo: •	Enrollment fees waived for the first 12 months •	Dedicated care management to help navigate your care and choose a primary care manager •	A 24/7 nurse advice line •	Routine vision coverage and preventive services •	Time and distance access standards •	Fewer out-of-pocket costs •	No claims to file when you see providers approved by the plan

TRICARE Prime: A managed care option offering the most affordable and comprehensive coverage.
TRICARE Prime Remote: A managed care option offering the most affordable and comprehensive coverage to active duty families in remote U.S. locations.
TRICARE Select: A preferred provider network available to all non-active duty beneficiaries. Most freedom of choice.
TRICARE Prime Overseas: A managed care option offering the most affordable and comprehensive coverage to active duty families living overseas.
TRICARE Prime Remote Overseas: A managed care option offering the most affordable and comprehensive coverage to active duty families in remote overseas locations.
TRICARE Select Overseas: TRICARE Select Overseas is a fee-for-service option, which allows you the most flexibility in getting care, but will cost you more out-of-pocket than one of the overseas Prime options.
TRICARE Reserve Select: TRICARE Reserve Select a premium-based health plan that qualified National Guard and Reserve members may purchase.
TRICARE Retired Reserve: TRICARE Retired Reserve is a premium-based health plan that qualified retired Reserve members and survivors may purchase.
TRICARE For Life: TRICARE For Life offers secondary coverage to TRICARE beneficiaries who are have both Medicare Part A & B.
TRICARE Young Adult - Select: A premium-based, worldwide health plan that qualified adult children of eligible sponsors may purchase.
TRICARE Young Adult - Prime Option: A premium-based, worldwide health plan that qualified adult children of eligible sponsors may purchase.
TRICARE Young Adult - Prime Option (Over: A premium-based, worldwide health plan that qualified adult children of eligible sponsors may purchase.
TRICARE Young Adult - Prime Option (Prim: A premium-based, worldwide health plan that qualified adult children of eligible sponsors may purchase.
TRICARE Young Adult - Prime Option (USFH: The US Family Health Plan is a TRICARE Prime option available through networks of community-based, not-for-profit health care systems in six areas of the United States.
US Family Health Plan: The US Family Health Plan is a TRICARE Prime option available through networks of community-based, not-for-profit health care systems in six areas of the United States.
TRICARE Prime Demo: The TRICARE Prime Demo is a TRICARE Prime option available in the Atlanta and Tampa metropolitan areas.
